memberdef.name: TEST_THRESHOLD
memberdef.initializer: 25 /* This is Rx FIFO WaterMark Threshold */

briefdescription.para: The main entry point for showing the usage of XCanPs driver in interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: The example configures the device for internal loop back mode, then sends multiple CAN frames and receives the same number of CAN frame's using the Rx Watermark Interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the CAN driver which is going to be connected to the interrupt controller.
contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, otherwise driver-specific error code.

- In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.
If the device is not working correctly, this function may enter an infinite loop and will never return to the caller.

detaileddescription.para: Baud Rate Prescaler Register (BRPR), Bit Timing Register (BTR) and RXFIFO Watermark Interrupt Register (RXWIR) are set in this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the driver instance.

simplesect.para: If the CAN device is not working correctly, this function may enter an infinite loop and will never return to the caller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Read the Received CAN frames from the FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the driver instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if all the CAN Frames are received and the data is the same as that was sent.
XST_FAILURE if the required number of CAN frames have not been received or if the Received Data is not the same as the data that was sent.

briefdescription.para: Callback function (called from interrupt handler) to handle confirmation of transmit events when in interrupt mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the interrupt handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.

simplesect.para: This function is called by the driver within interrupt context.

briefdescription.para: Callback function (called from interrupt handler) to handle frames received in interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function is called once all the frames are received.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the interrupt handler, which in our case is a pointer to the device instance.

simplesect.para: This function is called by the driver within interrupt context.

briefdescription.para: Callback function (called from interrupt handler) to handle error interrupt.

detaileddescription.para: Error code read from Error Status register is passed into this function

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the interrupt handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCANPS_ESR_* defined in xcanps_hw.h

simplesect.para: This function is called by the driver within interrupt context.

briefdescription.para: Callback function (called from interrupt handler) to handle the following interrupts:

listitem.para: XCANPS_IXR_TXBFLL_MASK: TX High Priority Buffer Full Interrupt

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed from the interrupt Handler, which in our case is a pointer to the driver instance.
is a bit mask indicating pending interrupts. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more of the XCANPS_IXR_*_MASK value(s) mentioned above.

simplesect.para: This function is called by the driver within interrupt context. This function needs to be changed to meet specific application needs.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the Can Rx Watermark interrupt example.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the example has completed successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the example has failed.

briefdescription.para: This example shows how to use the CAN driver/device in interrupt mode using the Rx Watermark Interrupt.

simplesect.para: The Baud Rate Prescaler Register (BRPR) and Bit Timing Register (BTR) are setup such that CAN baud rate equals 40Kbps, assuming that the the CAN clock is 24MHz. The user needs to modify these values based on the desired bau rate and the CAN clock frequency. For more information see the CAN 2.0A, CAN 2.0B, ISO 11898-1 specifications.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd/sv 01/12/10 First release 3.1 adk 10/11/15 Fixed CR#911958 Add support for Tx Watermark testing. 3.7 ht 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 07/10/23 Updated conditional macros for interrupt headers. 3.10 ht 12/13/24 Fix C++ compilation errors and warnings in SDT flow. 3.12 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
